mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and falling corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing sector that reduces consumer wealth and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns (interaction term coefficient insignificant at 90 percent confidence).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient manufacturing output that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods consistently precede negative 5-day SPX returns, reversing the predicted direction.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4 percent monthly forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises faster than 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate creates an optimal macro environment where expanding corporate profits are discounted at lower rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS levels yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces business investment and supply chain activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Two consecutive months of falling INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of historical cases.
